(brother)
[[Neil Fox (rugby league)|Neil Fox]] (brother) }} }} '''Peter Fox''' (30 March 1933 – 25 February 2019) was an English [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1950s and 1960s, and [[Coach (sport)|coach]]ed in the 1970s, 1980s and 1990s. He was the brother of [[Don Fox|Don]] and [[Neil Fox (rugby league)|Neil Fox]], and together they formed one of the legendary rugby league families. == Career == He had a 13-year playing career with; Sharlston Rovers, [[Featherstone Rovers]],Bailey, Ron (1956). ''The Official History Of Featherstone Rovers R.L.F.C.''. Wakefield Express. ASIN: B00O1TLDPC [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]], [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], [[Hunslet F.C. (1883)|Hunslet]] and [[Wakefield Trinity]], he became one of British rugby league's most successful coaches. Peter Fox was the [[Coach (sport)|coach]] in [[Featherstone Rovers]]' 33-14 victory over [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford Northern]] in the [[1973 Challenge Cup]] Final during the [[1972–73 Northern Rugby Football League season#Challenge Cup|1972–73 season]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 12 May 1973, in front of a crowd of 72,395, and was the [[Coach (sport)|coach]] in the 9-24 defeat by [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the [[1974 Challenge Cup]] Final during the [[1973–74 Northern Rugby Football League season#Challenge Cup|1973–74 season]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 11 May 1974, in front of a crowd of 77,400. [[Bramley RLFC|Bramley]] won promotion to Division One, under Peter Fox in the 1976-77 season. He coached Great Britain and England, which included beating the Australians in 1978. He coached [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford Northern]] in two spells one in 1977 where he had a very successful spell winning the championship in 1979-80 and 1980-81. He returned to Bradford Northern for a second spell in 1991, and in the [[1993–94 Rugby Football League season|1993-94 season]] Bradford Northern finish as runners-up in the Championship behind Wigan on points difference. He left the club in 1995. Fox also coached Yorkshire in the [[Rugby League War of the Roses|Rodstock War of the Roses]] series, winning all six games played against Lancashire between 1985 and 1991.{{cite news|last1=Fitzpatrick|first1=Paul|title=Fox in pursuit of sixth win|work=The Guardian|date=18 September 1991|location=London|page=17|id={{ProQuest|187193747}}}}{{cite news|last1=Fitzpatrick|first1=Paul|title=Ashton on the trail|work=The Guardian|date=19 September 1991|location=London|page=17|id={{ProQuest|187277308}}}} Fox appears to have scored no drop-goals (or field-goals as they are currently known in Australasia), but prior to the [[1974–75 Northern Rugby Football League season|1974–75 season]] all goals, whether; [[Try (rugby)|conversion]]s, [[Penalty (rugby)#Penalties in rugby league|penalties]], or [[Drop goal#Rugby league|drop-goal]]s, scored 2-points, consequently prior to this date drop-goals were often not explicitly documented, therefore '0' drop-goals may indicate drop-goals not recorded, rather than no drop-goals scored. ==Honoured at Featherstone Rovers== Peter Fox is a Featherstone Rovers Hall of Fame inductee.{{cite web|url=http://www.pontefractandcastlefordexpress.co.uk/news/featherstone-rovers-legends-added-to-hall-of-fame-1-8214653|title=Featherstone Rovers legends added to Hall of Fame|publisher=pontefractandcastlefordexpress.co.uk|date=5 November 2016|access-date=1 January 2017}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://web.archive.org/web/20080820100209/http://www.wordofsport.com/books/1903659396_peter_fox/ Peter Fox's biography at worldofsport.com] *[https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/coaches/Peter_Fox__GB_/summary.html Coach statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org] {{Featherstone Rovers - 1973 Challenge Cup Final winners}} {{Navboxes |title= Coaching positions |list1= {{England national rugby league team coaches}} {{Great Britain national rugby league team coaches}} {{Featherstone Rovers coaches}} {{Wakefield Trinity coaches}} {{Bramley RLFC coaches}} {{Bradford Bulls Coaches}} {{Leeds Rhinos coaches}} }} {{Featherstone Rovers Hall of Fame Inductees}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Fox, Peter}} [[Category:1933 births]] [[Category:2019 deaths]] [[Category:Batley Bulldogs players]] [[Category:Bradford Bulls coaches]] [[Category:Bramley R.L.F.C. coaches]] [[Category:England national rugby league team coaches]] [[Category:English rugby league coaches]] [[Category:English rugby league players]] [[Category:Featherstone Rovers coaches]] [[Category:Featherstone Rovers players]] [[Category:Great Britain national rugby league team coaches]] [[Category:Hull Kingston Rovers players]] [[Category:Hunslet F.C.
